description: The generic audit methodology specified by sl-auditor: the squeeze turned on a TARGET squeeze loop to decide whether it is actually sound or only looks sound. The auditor judges the target against an upper bound (the compliance contract C1-C4 + the stabilizer set the target is supposed to satisfy) and a lower bound (the target's inspected, executable reality: the sl_disjointness_check.py run as the mechanical floor, plus the barrier ablation and the seeded coherent-and-wrong probes). The target's own SL-1.0 self-description is read as a CLAIM to test against reality, never as ground truth; the auditor is barriered from the target's implementation internals and its actors' rationale and learns target behaviour by RUNNING the target's oracle. Dominant coherent-and-wrong: a target that is quietly collapsing into a rubber stamp (self-certifying actor, honorary barrier, self-declared done) while its self-description still reads as compliant. The one residual the auditor cannot self-certify — its own soft-vs-soft judgments (framing faithfulness, self-description accuracy) — is routed to a disjoint human / cross-provider base (Dimension 8). Generic: no domain, no instance (monitors: null; the bridge expands to a placeholder audited-loop).

sources.name: Compliance contract (C1-C4 + stabilizer set)
sources.notes: the fixed standard of soundness the target must satisfy; the auditor's and the disjoint base's upper bound

sources.name: sl_disjointness_check.py over the target's .sl.json (mechanical floor)
sources.notes: set-algebra check of self-certification / shared-evidence / barriers / endogeneity / terminus — a floor, not a ceiling

sources.name: Barrier ablation + seeded coherent-and-wrong probes + flag-rate band
sources.notes: the inspected, executable reality for the dimensions the checker cannot decide: does the catch rate move when the barrier is removed; are planted falsehoods caught; is each monitor in the discriminating band

sources.name: The target loop's SL-1.0 self-description (the claim under audit)
sources.notes: read as a CLAIM to test against reality (an org chart to distrust), never as ground truth; not a bound source

sources.name: The target loop's implementation internals + its actors' rationale
sources.notes: must stay inside the target; the auditor learns target behaviour by RUNNING its oracle, never by reading these — its forbidden move

sources.name: Per-dimension findings report (D1-D9, with severities)
sources.notes: the auditor's deliverable; loud-fail on any undecidable dimension, never a quiet pass

sources.name: Disjoint base judgment (cross-provider / human review)
sources.notes: closes the residual the auditor cannot self-certify; no executable refuter

actors.name: SL auditor (runs Dimensions 1-9)
actors.builds: the per-dimension findings report: ingest the target's .sl.json and run the mechanical floor (D1/D2/C2), then the manual dimensions (D3 oracle, D4 gates, D5 coherent-and-wrong seeding, D7 stabilizer ablation), routing D6 to sl-monitoring-sl and D8 to the disjoint base

upper_bound.text: the compliance contract C1-C4 + the stabilizer set the target is supposed to satisfy

lower_bound.text: the target's inspected, executable reality: the disjointness checker run plus the barrier ablation and the seeded coherent-and-wrong probes

forbidden_moves: self-certify its own soft-vs-soft residual (framing faithfulness, self-description accuracy)
pass an undecidable dimension silently instead of DEPENDENCY-UNMET / routing to the disjoint base
inflate the target's claimed soundness (safe direction: downgrade when unsure)
read the target's internals instead of running its oracle

actors.name: Bridge -> the audited loop (run / inspected as a sub-agent)
actors.builds: the audited loop collapsed to one row; forwards its SL-1.0 self-description registry and exposes its runnable oracles, never its implementation internals or its actors' rationale

upper_bound.text: the audited loop's own bounds (see its document)

lower_bound.text: the audited loop's own machine verdicts / oracles, exposed for the auditor to run

forbidden_moves: forward a self-description that flatters the apparatus (self-description drift)
present a stale self-description as current
expose internals in place of a runnable oracle

actors.name: Disjoint base (cross-provider / human reviewer, terminus)
actors.builds: the terminal review of what an internal audit cannot self-certify: soft-vs-soft faithfulness and self-description accuracy (Dimension 8)

upper_bound.text: the compliance contract: is the auditor's residual judgment genuinely faithful, or does it share the target's blind spot?

lower_bound.text: no executable oracle — soft-vs-soft faithfulness has no hard refuter; this absence is why the reviewer is the terminus
lower_bound.absent_reason: framing faithfulness / self-description accuracy is not mechanically decidable; closed by a disjoint cross-provider or human reviewer, not a machine

forbidden_moves: count a same-provider-family judge as full disjointness (that is only partial)
certify without first passing the known-contradiction / known-consistent controls

disjointness.rationale: The auditor judges the target only against the compliance contract (U) and the target's inspected, executable reality (L) — the disjointness-checker run, the barrier ablation, the seeded coherent-and-wrong probes — never against the target's account of itself: it reads the target's .sl.json as a CLAIM to test against reality, and is barriered from the target's implementation internals and its actors' rationale (it runs the target's oracle to learn behaviour). The auditor's own soft-vs-soft judgments (is the framing faithful? is the self-description accurate?) share the target's blind spot and are the one residual it cannot self-certify; that residual is closed by a disjoint cross-provider / human base, not by the auditor. An internal audit that claims to have certified everything has demonstrated the failure it was hunting for.
disjointness.load_bearing_barrier: the auditor never reads the target's implementation internals or its actors' rationale (it runs the target's oracle to learn behaviour), and never self-certifies its own soft-vs-soft residual — that is routed to the disjoint base

catchability.blind_spot: the loop is coherent-and-wrong ABOUT ITSELF — its self-description has drifted from its actual apparatus, or it is quietly collapsing into a rubber stamp (self-certifying actor, honorary barrier, self-declared done) while still reading as compliant
catchability.via: the mechanical floor (sl_disjointness_check.py) + barrier ablation (does the catch rate move?) + seeded coherent-and-wrong probes — inspected reality vs the claimed self-description

catchability.blind_spot: its own soft-vs-soft judgments — framing faithfulness, self-description accuracy — on which it shares the target's blind spot and cannot self-certify
catchability.via: external / cross-provider or human review (Dimension 8) — the disjoint base that closes the residual

disjointness.terminus: disjoint_base (cross-provider or human review closes the residual the auditor cannot self-certify; the automation alone does not)
